witnesses.description: A three-state absorbing chain with target state hit.
Exact absorption probabilities for the three-state chain.
Exact expected hitting times for the three-state chain.

checks.claim: The listed first-hit probabilities and survival mass are correct through horizon 4.
checks.notes: The validator carries only not-yet-hit mass forward and recomputes first-hit probabilities exactly.

checks.claim: The listed absorption probabilities satisfy the finite fixed-point equations.
checks.notes: The validator checks target states equal 1 and non-target rows equal transition-weighted successor probabilities.

checks.claim: The listed expected hitting times satisfy the finite linear equations.
checks.notes: The validator checks h(target)=0 and h(i)=1+sum_j P(i,j)h(j) for non-target states.

checks.claim: The finite first-hit table has survival mass P(T > 4) = 1/4.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes P(T > 4)=5/16 by carrying only not-yet-hit mass, rejecting the false 1/4 claim. The separate qf-lra-bad-survival-mass row owns the proof-object refutation.

checks.claim: The fixed malformed survival-mass claim is linearly inconsistent.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: The source SMT-LIB artifact isolates the final exact-linear contradiction after finite replay computes survival mass as 5/16.

checks.claim: The malformed expected hitting-time table satisfies the finite equations.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the start equation as 7/2, not 3. The separate qf-lra-bad-expected-time row owns the proof-object refutation.

checks.claim: The fixed malformed expected hitting-time equation is linearly inconsistent.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: The source SMT-LIB artifact isolates the exact-linear contradiction after finite replay computes the malformed start equation as 7/2.

checks.claim: General recurrence, transience, and stopping theorems require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: recurrence/transience classifications, infinite-horizon hitting probabilities, optional stopping, mixing bounds, and Markov-chain potential theory
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked; general hitting-time theory remains a proof-assistant horizon.
